hypothesis: Enhancing steric complementarity with SSTR2-unique ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions by introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will increase selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 binding while reducing off-target interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: the peptides can achieve strong SSTR2 binding and selectivity. However, structural quality must be improved to ensure the designs are physically plausible and can be synthesized or tested experimentally.

proposed_changes.rationale: Incorporate pLDDT into the objective function to prioritize candidates with better predicted structural quality, addressing the structural failure pattern.
Reduce the clash tolerance to enforce better geometry and reduce structural artifacts, which are likely contributing to the pLDDT=0.0 failures.
